title: Tick volume as order-flow proxy: practitioner evidence claims 90%+ correlation with real spot FX volume, but is contested and non-peer-reviewed
claim: The best-known evidence for tick volume validity (Marney 2011, practitioner research) claims 90%+ correlation between tick activity and actual traded spot-FX volume; a counter-source examining futures volume finds tick volume 'mostly uncorrelated'. Net: tick count is defensible only as a RELATIVE intra-day activity proxy, not as a magnitude measure — no peer-reviewed gold-specific validation was found.

source.label: Global Prime (Medium), 'Why Is Tick Volume Important To Monitor In The Forex Market?' (citing Caspar Marney, 2011)
source.quote: striking high levels of accuracy between tick activity and actual traded volume ... correlations of 90% plus

rule_formal: Use tick_count(minute) only as normalized relative measure: z-score vs same-minute-of-day baseline or fraction of day total; never cross-day absolute comparisons (broker-feed dependent).

numbers.effect: Claimed correlation 90%+ between tick activity and actual traded volume (spot FX, Marney 2011)
numbers.sample: Marney 2011 practitioner research (webinar/paper, non-peer-reviewed); pairs not specified on fetched page
numbers.caveats: COUNTER-EVIDENCE fetched from EarnForex guide (https://www.earnforex.com/guides/tick-volume-in-forex/), verbatim: 'Tick volume seems to be mostly uncorrelated with real volume in the futures market' and 'One tick (price update) may be caused by either a small volume trade or a very large trade'. Neither source is peer-reviewed; no gold-specific validation found; Dukascopy col-6 tick-count usable relative-only per project F6 caveat

mechanism: Not a forced-flow claim — a measurement-validity claim (Q5 explicitly asks this). Causal link: dealers refresh quotes more often when order flow hits them, so quote-update intensity co-moves with trading activity; but one update can represent any trade size, so intensity ≈ activity, not signed volume.
relevance: Q5 (core): licenses using our M1 tick-count as a relative activity/participation feature in sweep-vs-break tests (e.g. tick-count z-score during the breakout minute), while blocking any design that needs true volume magnitude or signed order flow.

test_design.lookahead_risk: Tick-count of the breakout minute and prior minutes is available at entry — no lookahead; same-minute-of-day normalization baseline must be computed from PAST days only

overlap_risk: Day-aggregated tick count will correlate strongly with rv (dead variable) — value, if any, must come from event-level relative spikes (breakout-minute vs baseline), not day sums
